Facts.text: Signing of the Treaty of Saint-Clair-sur-Epte between Charles the Simple and Viking leader Rollo of Normandy creates the Duchy of Normandy
Siege of Shirakawa-den in Japan
Khwarezmian Tatars sack Jerusalem, decimating the city's Christian population and driving out Jews
Charles IV of Luxembourg is elected Emperor of the Holy Roman Empire
Heir to the Bohemian throne elected German anti-king Charles IV
Chinese fleet commander Zheng He sets sail to the Spice Islands on his first major expedition, leading 208 vessels, including 62 treasure ships with 27,800 sailors
Arnold van Egmont becomes Duke of Gelre
Giuliano della Rovere is appointed bishop of Coutances
Trial against "heretic" John Pistorius at The Hague
The Convocation of the Clergy subscribes to the Ten Articles, beliefs of the English Church underHenry VIII
English explorer Martin Frobisher sights Greenland
French King Henri III accepts the demands of the Catholic League
Armies of Savoy, Mantua, and Parma occupy Milan

The first Quakers to land in Boston, Englishwomen Ann Austin and Mary Fisher, are arrested and jailed by the Puritan colonial government. After 5 weeks of imprisonment, they are deported back to Barbados.
City of Vilnius is recaptured from its occupying Muscovite force by the army of the Grand Duchy led by Michał Kazimierz Pac
Netherlands and Denmark sign a defense treaty
Battle of Oudenaarde, Spanish Netherlands: Grand Alliance beats France
Mathematical calculations suggest that on this day in history, Pluto moved from the ninth to the eighth most distant "planet" from the Sun for the last time before 1979
Jews are expelled from Little Russia by order of Tsarina Anne
Thomas Hutchins is designated Geographer of the US
Prussian army moves into French territory
French astronomer Jean-Louis Pons discovers his first comet
US invades Canada (Detroit frontier)
Edmund Hickly achieves the first known 10-wicket innings (Kent vs. England)
London's Waterloo Station opens
Big Ben's second and current bell first chimes inside the Clock Tower
Battle of Laurel Mountain, Virginia - General Morris forces retreat of rebels
Battle of Rich Mountain, Virginia - Rosecrans forces rebels to surrender
Battle of Green River, Kentucky (Morgan's Ohio Raid)
Japanese battle cruiser shoots at Dutch warship Medusa, killing four
US military holds first draft lottery in New York City; exemptions are offered at cost of $300, a fee only the wealthy could afford
Confederate forces led by General Jubal Early begin invasion of Washington, D.C. with the Battle of Fort Stevens
Kate Edger becomes New Zealand’s first woman graduate and first woman in the British Empire to earn a Bachelor of Arts
British fleet bombards Alexandria, Egypt
Pennsylvania's Monongahela River rises 32 feet (9.7 meters) after 24 hours of rainfall
Tijuana in Mexico becomes a city
A revolution led by the liberal general and politician, José Santos Zelaya, takes over state power in Nicaragua
The first cultured pearl is obtained by Kōkichi Mikimoto in Japan
French film pioneers Auguste and Louis Lumière show their film to scientists
Boer General De la Rey captures Scots Greys and Lincolns
Forest fire destroys Canadian town of South Porcupine, Ontario, killing at least 70 people
There are six medallists in the Stockholm Olympic pole vault: American Harry Babcock takes gold (3.95 m), countrymen Frank Nelson and Marc Wright tie for silver, and three-man tie for bronze
German cruiser Königsberg sinks off Dar-es-Salam
Battle of Verdun: Germany launches an offensive on Fort Souville in France but is defeated by artillery and machine gunners
US Congress passes Federal Aid Road Act
Dutch Second Chamber approves 8-hour workday and no Sunday work
East and West Prussia vote in a plebiscite to become part of Germany, though part of West Prussia is handed to Poland to provide a "Polish Corridor"
Mongolia gains independence from China (National Day)
The Hollywood Bowl opens in Los Angeles
Muslim-Hindu rebellion in Delhi, India
NY Giants beat Phillies 23-8
Triborough Bridge linking Manhattan, the Bronx, and Queens opens
British and German dogfight above Lyme Bay
Massacres of Poles in Volhynia
Seventh day of Battle of Kursk, USSR: Operation Citadel continues
US 45th Division occupies airport Comiso Sicily
US 82nd Airborne division shot at by "friendly fire" in Sicily
Franklin Rooseveltannounces he will run for a fourth term as President of the United States
Kingman Douglass ends term as deputy director of CIA
First air bombing of Jerusalem
114°F (45.6°C), Sedgwick, Colorado (ties state record; broken in 2019)
First White Citizens' Council is organized in Indianola, Mississippi
Congress authorizes all US currency to say "In God We Trust"
New USAF Academy dedicated at Lowry AFB in Colorado with 300 cadets
Czechoslovakia adopts Constitution
Ivory Coast, Dahomey, Upper Volta, and Niger declare independence
Moise Tsjombe declares Congolese county Katanga independent
Gene Kiniski defeats Verne Gagne in Minneapolis to become NWA champion
Brothers Hank and Tommie Aaron hit home runs in the same inning
Cosmonaut Nikolayev sets the record for the longest spaceflight at four days
First transatlantic TV transmission via satellite (Telstar I)
Fred Baldasare is first to swim English Channel underwater (scuba)
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Christmas Island
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
South African ANC members Walter Sisulu, Andrew Mlangeni, and Govan Mbeki arrested
TV quiz show "The Newlywed Game," hosted by Bob Eubanks, premieres in the US on ABC
Rolling Stones release "Honky Tonk Woman"
Chilean parliament nationalizes American copper mines
Phillies' Deron Johnson hits three home runs, completing a streak of four consecutive home runs over two games
The Irish Republican Army set off a number of bombs in the center of Belfast, injuring several people
Brazilian Boeing 707 crashes near Paris, killing 122
US House Judiciary Committee releases evidence on Watergate inquiry
World Football League kicks off debut season, with first games played in Orlando, Philadelphia, Chicago, Memphis, and Birmingham
First US football club in Austria forms (FAAFC - first Austrian American)
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
In pre-game promo at Atlanta County Stadium, 34 couples wed at home plate followed by Championship Wrestling "Headlocks & Wedlocks"
Auto with liquid gas crashes and explodes in Spain, killing 160
US Skylab enters atmosphere over Australia and disintegrates
American hostage Richard Queen freed by Iran militants due to illness
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Neva Rockefeller is first woman ordered to pay her husband alimony
FIFA World Cup Final, Santiago Bernabéu, Madrid, Spain: Italy beats West Germany 3-1 in front of 90,000
Britain's MusicBox begins satellite transmission to Europe
Lucas Mangope re-elected president of Bophuthatswana
US Government mandates that all cars must be equipped with airbags or seatbelts by 1989
Refurbished space shuttle Columbia moves overland from Palmdale to Dryden
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Mary Beth Whitehead christens surrogate Baby M, Sara
Heart's "Alone" single goes to #1 for three weeks
Orioles' Cal Ripkin becomes first to manage two sons as Billy joins Cal
President Ronald Reagan sportscasts All-Star Game
NYC police arrest "Dartman" (stabbed over 50 women with darts)
Oka Crisis: First Nations land dispute in Quebec begins between Mohawk people and town of Oka
Nigeria Airways DC-8 crashes at Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, killing all 261 passengers and crew on board
Total solar eclipse is seen in Hawaii
Iraq resumes oil exports, ending a 5-week halt in protest of a US and British-sponsored UN Security Council resolution
209 people are killed in a series of bomb attacks in Mumbai, India
15th ESPY Awards: LaDainian Tomlinson and Taryne Mowatt win
FIFA World Cup Final, Soccer City, Johannesburg, South Africa: Andrés Iniesta scores an extra-time winner as Spain beats the Netherlands, 1-0 for first World Cup title
Neptune completes its first orbit since its discovery on September 23, 1846
Police academy suicide bombing kills 20 in Sana'a, Yemen
S/2012 P 1, the fifth moon of Pluto, is discovered
Thirty people are killed in a wave of bomb and gun attacks across Iraq
The UN Security Council calls for a special meeting to discuss the current Israel–Palestinian conflict; Israel continue attacks on Gaza
Oldest stone tools outside Africa are discovered in Lantian County, western China, estimated to be 2.12 million years old and made by hominins
Japan’s Hayabusa2 spacecraft lands on the Ryugu asteroid 300 million km (185 million miles) from Earth for a second time to collect samples
Last models of Volkswagen's Beetle car are produced in Puebla, Mexico, ending production worldwide after 80 years
US stock markets reach new records, the Dow tops 27,000 points for the first time, and the S&P 500 hits 3,000 points
Rare mass anti-government protests across Cuba due to economic hardships and effects of COVID-19 lead to widespread arrests
New study suggests dogs can "see" with their noses, the first documentation of a direct connection between the olfactory bulb and occipital lobe in any species
Heavy rains in northern India result in 20 deaths, with Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand states experiencing landslides and flash floods, closing over 700 roads

Scientists select Crawford Lake, Ontario, as the reference location for the beginning of a new interval of geologic time, the human-caused Anthropocene Epoch, starting in the 1950s and ending the Holocene; the proposal is later rejected
Cole Escola's stage comedy "Oh, Mary!" opens at the Lyceum Theatre, NYC
Tijuana, Mexico, sets new Guinness World Record for largest margarita at 34,419 liters (9,092.5 gallons)
Antoninus PiussucceedsHadrianas Emperor of Rome
Battle of the Golden Spurs (Guldensporenslag in Dutch) near Kortrijk, Belgium: Flemish coalition defeat the French army ofPhilip IV
PopeClement VIIexcommunicates England's KingHenry VIII
Samuel de Champlainreturns to Quebec
Oxford mathematicianJohn Wallisgives a lecture on Euclid’s parallel postulate, first Western attempt to derive the parallel postulate as a theorem
Battle of Drogheda Boyne: KingWilliam IIIdefeats deposed KingJames IIof England
French KingLouis XVIdismisses finance ministerJacques Necker, sparking riots in Paris
US Marine Corps formally established as a distinct military branch by an Act of Congress signed by PresidentJohn Adams
Vice President of the United StatesAaron Burrmortally wounds former Secretary of the TreasuryAlexander Hamiltonin a pistol duel
Italian scientistAmedeo Avogadropublishes his memoir on the molecular content of gases
English poetJohn Keatswrites "In the Cottage Where Burns is Born," "Lines Written in the Highlands," and "Gadfly"
Abraham Lincolnappoints General Henry Halleck general-in-chief of Union forces in US Civil war
US Patent Office states Joseph Swan rather thanThomas Edisoninvented the electric light carbon for the incandescent lamp
Wilfrid Lauriersworn in as the seventh Prime Minister of Canada
Charlotte Cooperbeats Hélène Prévost to become the first female Olympic tennis champion and the first individual female Olympic champion in any sport
British Prime MinisterLord Salisburyresigns from his third term in office due to ill health
Black intellectuals and activists led byW.E.B. Du Boisorganize the civil rights Niagara Movement
Factory worker Grace Brown is murdered by her boyfriend Chester Gillette at Big Moose Lake, New York, later a celebrated case that inspiresTheodore Dreiser's novel "An American Tragedy"
Future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerBabe Ruthmakes his MLB debut as a pitcher for the Boston Red Sox and earns a 4-3 win against the Cleveland Naps at Fenway Park
Harry Frazeesells the baseball team Red Sox to Ohio businessmen for $1 million
QueenWilhelmina of the Netherlandsappoints Hendrikus Colijn of the Anti-Revolutionary Party as head of the Dutch government
FDRbecomes the first US President to travel through the Panama Canal
MarshallPhilippe Pétain, French hero of World War I, becomes head of the Vichy collaborationist government of France
Counterattack byHermann Goering's armored division in Sicily, Italy
GeneralEisenhowernominated as Republican US presidential candidate
"To Kill a Mockingbird" byHarper Leeis first published by J. B. Lippincott & Co.
Israeli Mapai party nominatesDavid Ben-Gurion
Kenny Rogersforms the First Edition
Earl WeaverreplacesHank Baueras manager of Orioles
Start ofColin Cowdrey's 100th Test, first person to reach the milestone
David Bowiereleases the single "Space Oddity" nine days before Apollo 11 lands on the moon
US Medal of Freedom awarded posthumously to RevMartin Luther King Jr.
Sebastian Coeof UK sets a world record for 1,000 m (2:12.18)
Astros'Nolan Ryanbecomes the first to strike out 4,000 batters (Mets' Danny Heep)
Ingrid Kristiansenof Norway runs 10,000 m in a world record time of 30:13.74
Mike Tysonhires Donald Trump as an advisor
Presidential candidateRoss Perot, during an NAACP speech, refers to the audience as "you people"
More than 8,000 Bosnian Muslim men and boys are massacred by Bosnian Serbs after they overrun the UN "safe haven" of Srebrenica on directive ofRadovan Karadžić
Shaggyreleases his third studio album "Bombastic" (Grammy Award Best Reggae Album)
"Harry Potterand the Order of the Phoenix," the fifth film based on the books byJ. K. Rowling, is released
Future Basketball Hall of Fame guardSteve Nashis traded by the Phoenix Suns to the Los Angeles Lakers
Twentieth ESPY Awards:LeBron Jamesand Brittney Griner win
"Orange Is the New Black" starring Taylor Schilling premieres on Netflix, the first series to be nominated for both comedy and drama Emmy awards
BillionaireRichard Bransonflies to the edge of space on his Virgin Galactic rocket plane in a space tourism test
NASA and US PresidentJoe Bidenreveal first image from James Webb Space Telescope, a cluster of galaxies called SMACS 0723, 4 billion light-years away, as they appeared 13 billion years ago
UK Olympic gold medalist SirMo Farahreveals he was trafficked into the UK as a child, from Djibouti, to work as a domestic servant
Salomon August Andréeand crew leave Spitsbergen in northern Norway by balloon, heading for the North Pole
Mexican drug lordJoaquín "El Chapo" Guzmánescapes from Altiplano maximum-security prison west of Mexico City through a specially constructed 1.5 km tunnel from his cell to a nearby house
Bardaisan [Bardesanes], early Syrian Christian theologist, born in Edessa, Upper Mesopotamia (d. c. 222)
Anne of Bohemia, Queen of England and wife of Richard II, born in Prague, Kingdom of Bohemia (d. 1394)
Adolf van Nassau, German son of William I and Juliana of Stolberg, born in Dillenburg, Germany (d. 1568)
Robert Greene, English writer (Friar Bacon), born in Norwich, England (d. 1592)
Luis de Góngora, Spanish poet and writer, born in Córdoba, Spain (d. 1627)

Frederick I, King of Prussia (1701-13), born in Königsberg, Prussia (d. 1713)

Charles-Antoine Coypel, French carpet designer, born in Paris (d. 1752)

Eugène Simonis, Belgian sculptor, born in Liège, Belgium (d. 1882)

Charilaos Trikoupis, Greek politician (Prime Minister of Greece for 7 times from 1875-1895), born in Nafplion, Greece (d. 1896)

William Paul Roberts, Brigadier General (Confederate Army), born in Gates County, North Carolina (d. 1910)

Annie Armstrong, American missionary leader, born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1938)

Johannes van Laar, Dutch chemist (thermodynamics), born in The Hague, Netherlands (d. 1938)

Liza Lehmann, English composer, born in London, England (d. 1918)

Percy McAlister, Australian cricket batsman (8 Tests; Big Six cricket dispute 1912), born in Williamstown, Australia (d. 1938)
Stjepan Radic, Croatian politician (founder of Croatian People's Peasant Party), born in Desno Trebarjevo, Austria-Hungary (d. 1928)
Rollin Henry White, American inventor of steam boilers for early automobiles and Cletrac tractors, born in Cleveland (d. 1962)[1]
